name: Flipkart-Style Product Search Engine
scenario: Build the backend of Flipkart's product search and recommendation system using all four functional interfaces. Products are filtered with Predicates, transformed with Functions, displayed with Consumers, and fetched lazily with Suppliers. The design makes the search engine configurable ‚ÄĒ you swap the filter/sorter/formatter without changing the core logic. This is the Strategy Pattern expressed through functional interfaces.
description: A configurable product search engine where all search criteria, transformations, and output formatting are injected as functional interfaces. Demonstrates the real-world power of Predicate, Function, Consumer, Supplier and their compositions.

core.description: `Product` class: id, name, category, price (double), rating (double), inStock (boolean). All getters.

`SearchCriteria` class with Predicate fields: `inStockOnly` (filters out-of-stock), `minRating(double r)` (rating >= r), `maxPrice(double p)` (price <= p), `byCategory(String c)` (case-insensitive). Each as a static factory method returning a Predicate.

`ProductFormatter` class with Function<Product, String> fields (as static factory methods): `brief()` returns 'name ‚āĻprice', `detailed()` returns 'name | category | ‚āĻprice | rating‚ėÖ | stock-status'.

`SearchEngine.search(Supplier<List<Product>> catalog, Predicate<Product> filter, Function<Product, String> formatter, Consumer<String> output)` ‚ÄĒ runs the search pipeline: fetch catalog (Supplier), filter (Predicate), format (Function), output (Consumer).
Demonstrate three searches: (1) all Electronics in-stock ‚Č§‚āĻ2000 brief, (2) Books with rating ‚Č•4.0 detailed, (3) combined predicate: in-stock AND rating ‚Č•4.0 AND price ‚Č§‚āĻ5000.
Supplier is used for the catalog ‚ÄĒ the search() method calls supplier.get() when it needs data, demonstrating lazy access.
Use method references where appropriate (`System.out::println` for Consumer, `String::toUpperCase` etc.).

bonus.description: Add `SearchCriteria.nameContains(String keyword)` ‚ÄĒ case-insensitive keyword in name. Combine with .and() for multi-keyword search.
Add `Comparator<Product>` via lambda to the search engine ‚ÄĒ results sorted before output.
Add `Function<List<Product>, String> summaryFormatter` ‚ÄĒ takes the final result list and produces a one-line summary: 'Found N products. Price range: ‚āĻmin-‚āĻmax. Avg rating: X.X'

data_to_use:
Products (8 items):
- Electronics: Mouse ‚āĻ799 ‚ėÖ4.2 in-stock, Keyboard ‚āĻ1999 ‚ėÖ4.5 in-stock, Monitor ‚āĻ18999 ‚ėÖ4.7 out-of-stock, Headphones ‚āĻ2999 ‚ėÖ4.1 in-stock
- Books: Java Guide ‚āĻ649 ‚ėÖ4.3 in-stock, Python Cookbook ‚āĻ549 ‚ėÖ4.0 in-stock, Design Patterns ‚āĻ899 ‚ėÖ4.6 in-stock, Microservices ‚āĻ749 ‚ėÖ3.8 out-of-stock

criteria: brief() and detailed() as Function<Product, String> (20 pts)
All three searches produce correct results (15 pts)
At least 2 method references used (10 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The key design: SearchCriteria methods return `Predicate<Product>` (not `boolean`). The caller composes them: `SearchCriteria.inStockOnly().and(SearchCriteria.maxPrice(2000)).and(SearchCriteria.byCategory('Electronics'))`. The search() method signature uses all four interfaces.
submission_instructions: Save as ProductSearch.java. Run all 3 searches. Push to GitHub ‚ÄĒ this is real search engine architecture built from functional interfaces.
tips_for_success: SearchCriteria methods: `static Predicate<Product> inStockOnly() { return p -> p.isInStock(); }`
SearchEngine.search(): loop catalog from supplier.get(), test each with filter.test(), format with formatter.apply(), output with output.accept()
ProductFormatter: `static Function<Product, String> brief() { return p -> p.getName() + ' ‚āĻ' + p.getPrice(); }`
concepts_reinforced: Predicate as configurable filter condition (factory methods)
All four interfaces composing in a search pipeline
